target: WIN-029 detail_evidence: add averaging-convention disclosure for H_eff ~3316 km (ISS-2290 c5 hole 2 carry-over)

patches.find_pattern: (H_eff ≈ 3,316 km linear radial average; see WIN-002)
patches.replace_pattern: (H_eff ≈ 3,316 km linear radial average; the area-weighted average — proper for a disc per WIN-002 — gives H_eff ≈ 2,140 km → 35 Hz; either convention fails to match the observed 7.83 Hz)
patches.description: ISS-2290: The linear radial average and area-weighted average both fail the 7.83 Hz match, but a defender could argue cherry-picking. Adding the area-weighted result inside the parenthetical forecloses that attack. The two h_eff values (3316 km linear, 2140 km area-weighted) and derived frequencies (22.6 Hz, 35 Hz) are sourced from WIN-002's own analysis. Both choices are wrong by 3-4x.

tldr_verdict: ISS-2290 resolved: averaging-convention choice disclosed in-line. Both conventions (linear radial 3316 km → 22.6 Hz; area-weighted 2140 km → 35 Hz) fail the 7.83 Hz test by 3-4x — defender cherry-pick attack preempted.
notes: WIN-029.c5 also has a major hole (hole 1): tldr_evidence says '5.3%, 10.8%, and 6.4%' while tldr_verdict says '5.3%, 6.4%, and 12.1%' — a direct internal contradiction. This was NOT assigned to baby (no ISS filed). Flagging for decider awareness; it pre-dates this run and is a curmudgeon-cycle artefact that needs a separate ISS and patch.
